pages.raw_text:
Mrs. Philip P. Stafford spoke for
equal suffrage. She pleaded that
women be placed on an equal foot
ing with men politically as well as
industrially, and that then women
would be found shoulder to shoulder
with union labor, working for the
common good of all. She also en-
tered a plea for home for union
men in Spokane.


r. B. Martin, representing the
farmers, who have recently Joined
hands with organized labor, spoke
for clean government and honesty
in office. William J. Coates pre-
sided over the meeting. 49,


After the speaking the following
contests and prize winners were an
nounced at the park:


Baby contest-Winners with their
mothers: First, Mrs. T. j. Skillman,
baby Pauline, IL months old; Sec
ond, Mrs. H. Johnson, baby Joseph,
four months old; third, Mrs. M. O.
Ebert, baby Norman, 10 months old.
Prizes we're $40. Sc and 81.


Pie eating contest-Victor Nelson
first, Jack Quinten second. Willie
Van Buskirk third. Prizes $3.50, 81
and SO cents.


Pole climbing contest-George
Kratz first, 16 seconds; Fred Clio,
